userStory: As a customer, I want to open the Login page from the Home page and sign in with approved credentials, so that I can access authenticated account features and logged-in functionality.
applicationContext: WashTab is a single page application (SPA).
entryConditions: Verify the user is unauthenticated before starting the workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: As a customer, I want to open the Login page from the Home page and sign in with approved credentials, so that I can access authenticated account features and logged-in functionality.
WashTab is a single page application (SPA).
The workflow begins at the exact URL:
The Login page is not directly accessed by URL in this workflow and must be opened through the Home page person/profile button.
Successful authentication returns the user to `home_page` in authenticated state.
behaviorRules: The Login page must be opened through the `home_page` person/profile button for this workflow.
Password input must be masked by default.
Valid credentials must authenticate the user successfully.
Invalid credentials must not authenticate the user.
Authentication failures, including blank username, blank password, partial credentials, and incorrect credentials, must display only the generic `Failed` message.
Separate required-field validation messaging must not be displayed.
Failed authentication must maintain unauthenticated state.
Leading and trailing whitespace handling must follow implemented application behavior and be observationally validated.
Back and Home buttons on the Login page must return the user to `home_page` without authentication.
Successful authentication must return the user to `home_page` in authenticated state.
validationExpectations: The Login page opens successfully from `home_page`.
Username, Password, and Login controls are visible and interactive.
Back and Home buttons are visible and functional.
Password input remains masked during entry.
Invalid credential submissions display the generic `Failed` message.
Blank credential submissions display the generic `Failed` message.
Partial credential submissions display the generic `Failed` message.
No dedicated required-field validation message is displayed.
Failed authentication does not transition the application into authenticated state.
Successful authentication using approved credentials returns the user to authenticated `home_page`.
Authenticated user identity and authenticated-only features are visible after successful login.
Whitespace-modified credential handling is validated according to implemented application behavior.
transitionExpectations: Clicking the person/profile button transitions the application from `home_page` in `guest` state to the Login page.
Successful authentication transitions the application from the Login page to `home_page` in authenticated state.
Failed authentication keeps the user on the Login page or otherwise maintains unauthenticated state.
Invalid, blank, partial, and whitespace-modified credential failures must not transition the application into authenticated state.
Back button navigation returns the user from the Login page to `home_page` in unauthenticated state.
Home button navigation returns the user from the Login page to `home_page` in unauthenticated state.
Independent negative and return-navigation branches must begin from the canonical `home_page` `guest` entry context.
The unauthenticated entry state must be restored before executing each independent branch.
Transition from `home_page` to the Login page.
Validate Login page controls and behaviors.
Submit authentication using the Login button.
reuseGuidance: Reuse the existing Login page resource for:

target.name: home_page
target.url: Invalid, blank, partial, and whitespace-modified credential failures must not transition the application into authenticated state.

targetSignals.value: Invalid, blank, partial, and whitespace-modified credential failures must not transition the application into authenticated state.
Independent negative and return-navigation branches must begin from the canonical `home_page` `guest` entry context.

Verify Person Profile Button Visible And Enabled
Verify Back Button Visible And Enabled

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Login page controls are visible and enabled after navigation from home_page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Login page displays interactive Username, Password, Login, Back, and Home controls after navigation from home_page.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify password field masks characters during manual entry
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Password field masks entered characters during input and does not display the plaintext password value on screen.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify successful authentication using approved credentials returns the user to authenticated home_page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application authenticates the user, returns to home_page in authenticated state, and authenticated user identity display and authenticated-only functionality become visible.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify invalid username with valid password displays only the generic Failed message
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no dedicated required-field validation message is shown, and the user remains unauthenticated on the Login page or in unauthenticated state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify valid username with invalid password displays only the generic Failed message
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, the application does not transition to authenticated state, and no separate required-field validation message appears.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify invalid username and invalid password combination does not authenticate the user
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, only the generic Failed message is visible, and the user remains unauthenticated without returning to authenticated home_page.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify blank Username and blank Password submission displays only the generic Failed message
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no dedicated required-field validation message appears, and the application remains in unauthenticated state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify blank Username with populated Password displays only the generic Failed message
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, only the generic Failed message is displayed, and the application does not transition into authenticated state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify populated Username with blank Password displays only the generic Failed message
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, only the generic Failed message is displayed, and the user remains unauthenticated without dedicated required-field validation messaging.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify leading whitespace in Username credential submission follows implemented application behavior
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application behavior for leading whitespace in credentials is observationally validated, and if authentication fails the generic Failed message is the only authentication error shown while the application remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify trailing whitespace in Password credential submission follows implemented application behavior
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application behavior for trailing whitespace in credentials is observationally validated, and any authentication failure displays only the generic Failed message while maintaining unauthenticated state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify whitespace-only credential submission does not authenticate the user
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The user is not authenticated, the application remains in unauthenticated state, and only the generic Failed message is displayed if validation feedback appears.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Back button returns the user from Login page to unauthenticated home_page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns the user to home_page in unauthenticated guest state without logging in the user.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home button returns the user from Login page to unauthenticated home_page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns the user to home_page in unauthenticated guest state and no authenticated user functionality is displayed.

validations: The Login page displays interactive Username, Password, Login, Back, and Home controls after navigation from home_page.
The Password field masks entered characters during input and does not display the plaintext password value on screen.
The application authenticates the user, returns to home_page in authenticated state, and authenticated user identity display and authenticated-only functionality become visible.

Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no dedicated required-field validation message is shown, and the user remains unauthenticated on the Login page or in unauthenticated state.
Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, the application does not transition to authenticated state, and no separate required-field validation message appears.
Authentication fails, only the generic Failed message is visible, and the user remains unauthenticated without returning to authenticated home_page.
Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no dedicated required-field validation message appears, and the application remains in unauthenticated state.
Authentication fails, only the generic Failed message is displayed, and the application does not transition into authenticated state.
Authentication fails, only the generic Failed message is displayed, and the user remains unauthenticated without dedicated required-field validation messaging.

The application behavior for leading whitespace in credentials is observationally validated, and if authentication fails the generic Failed message is the only authentication error shown while the application remains unauthenticated.

The application behavior for trailing whitespace in credentials is observationally validated, and any authentication failure displays only the generic Failed message while maintaining unauthenticated state.
The user is not authenticated, the application remains in unauthenticated state, and only the generic Failed message is displayed if validation feedback appears.
The application returns the user to home_page in unauthenticated guest state without logging in the user.
The application returns the user to home_page in unauthenticated guest state and no authenticated user functionality is displayed.

wrapperExpectations: Reuse approved shared/common wrapper keywords before low-level SeleniumLibrary actions.
Do not invent suite-level custom keywords; compose from approved upstream resource knowledge.

mustNotInvent: duplicate ownership for controls already owned in approved upstream resources
hardcoded workflow routing or mapped page logic in Python
downstreamGuidance.generationRule: Downstream generation must consume approved workflow knowledge plus approved resource context before creating new artifacts.

unresolvedGaps: The Login page Username field required for authentication workflows is missing from the approved element set.
The Login page Password field required for authentication and password masking validation is missing from the approved element set.
The Login button used to submit authentication is missing from the approved element set.
The workflow explicitly requires validation of the generic 'Failed' authentication message, but no validation/message element exists in the approved model.
Customer signup button belongs to the upstream home_page workflow and is unrelated to the login_page workflow scope.
Clean survey button is unrelated to the login workflow and should not be owned by login_page.
Site survey button is unrelated to the login workflow and should not be modeled as a login_page element.
The Home button already exists as a shared upstream navigation control and is duplicated by a generic anchor element.
Locator xpath=//a is dangerously generic and likely to match multiple links across the application.
Element naming exposes raw DOM ids instead of business-readable semantics.
Element naming is implementation-centric rather than workflow-centric.
Element naming exposes technical DOM identifiers and the control is not relevant to login workflow validation.
Element naming exposes raw implementation details instead of reusable navigation semantics.
Background container is decorative/layout-only and provides no meaningful automation value.
The workflow requires authenticated-state validation after successful login, but no authenticated user identity display or authenticated-only feature area elements are modeled.
